ontology.foodex2_description: Wheat flour tortilla / soft wrap — classified under flatbreads made from wheat flour; FoodEx2 code A000E used as closest defensible anchor for wheat-based flatbread.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No dedicated FoodEx2 code exists specifically for plain flour wrap; A000E (wheat bread/flatbread category) is the closest defensible anchor. Reviewer should confirm against EFSA FoodEx2 browser.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for plain flour wrap; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One medium plain flour wrap (approximately 45 g) is a typical single serving used as a wrap base for fillings such as grilled chicken, salad, or vegetables.

nutrient_source.title: Plain Wrap (Flour Tortilla) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for plain wrap (soft flour tortilla without filling), cr_id F05030011. All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this record with confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: Tortilla, wheat flour — Glycemic Index entry; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 30 is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database and the International Tables of Glycemic Index (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care) for wheat flour tortilla. Plain wrap is a soft wheat flour tortilla without filling; this is the closest approved analogous food. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (30) × carbohydrate per serving (55.97 g/100g × 45 g serving = 25.2 g) / 100 = 7.5.

hypertension.key_factor: Very high sodium content at 778 mg per 100 g, well above the 600 mg/100 g threshold for hypertension concern.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit plain wraps due to the very high sodium content (778 mg/100 g); choose low-sodium wrap alternatives (<200 mg/100 g) and pair with potassium-rich fillings such as avocado or spinach if consumed occasionally.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate content (55.97 g/100 g) with low dietary fibre (2.7 g/100 g), resulting in a high glycaemic load when consumed in standard portions.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should limit plain wraps made from refined wheat flour; choose wholemeal or high-fibre wrap variants (fibre >5 g/100 g) and fill with protein and non-starchy vegetables to reduce the overall glycaemic load of the meal.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium (778 mg/100 g) combined with refined carbohydrate dominance and moderate fat content raises cardiovascular risk, particularly for blood pressure and endothelial function.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit plain wrap consumption due to its high sodium content (778 mg/100 g), which contributes to elevated blood pressure; opt for wholegrain, low-sodium alternatives and use the wrap as a vehicle for heart-healthy fillings rich in unsaturated fats and vegetables.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High sodium (778 mg/100 g) may exacerbate joint swelling; refined flour provides no anti-inflammatory nutrients such as omega-3, polyphenols, or meaningful whole-food fibre.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should limit plain wraps due to high sodium content that may worsen tissue swelling; choose wholemeal wraps with added seeds (flaxseed, sesame) for anti-inflammatory benefit and pair with omega-3-rich fillings.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Plain wrap is a soft, pliable flatbread made from refined wheat flour, water, fat (typically vegetable shortening or oil), salt, and leavening agents. It is commercially produced and baked, resulting in a thin, flexible disc approximately 20–25 cm in diameter. The texture is soft and slightly chewy, with a mild, neutral flavour that makes it a versatile base for a wide range of fillings including grilled meats, salad vegetables, cheese, and sauces. Plain wraps are widely used in Singapore and globally as a convenient meal component for breakfast, lunch, and snack occasions. Nutritionally, plain wrap is carbohydrate-dominant (55.97 g/100 g) with moderate protein (7.9 g/100 g) and fat (6.8 g/100 g). The dietary fibre content is low at 2.7 g/100 g, and the sodium content is very high at 778 mg/100 g, which is a significant clinical concern for individuals with hypertension, cardiovascular disease, and rheumatoid arthritis. The glycemic index is approximately 30 (low), but the high carbohydrate load per serving means the glycemic load is moderate to high depending on portion size. Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should prefer wholemeal or high-fibre wrap variants. Plain wrap contains gluten and wheat allergens and is not suitable for those with coeliac disease or wheat allergy.

Is wrap bread high in sodium?
Is flour tortilla the same as a plain wrap?
How much carbohydrate is in a wrap?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F05030011) with confidence 0.95. GI sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2008) Diabetes Care for wheat flour tortilla as the closest approved analogous food. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for wheat flour tortilla (USDA SR Legacy #18364) as HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown; this is documented in notes.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (2.5 g/100 g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry #18364 (Tortillas, ready-to-bake or -fry, flour) as the HPB Foodo source does not report saturated fat breakdown for this item. This is an approved secondary source adaptation. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient, and moisture data for this specific HPB plain wrap entry are not reported in the HPB Foodo database as of 2026-04-16. USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy #18364 was consulted for partial micronutrient data but values were not mapped to avoid source mixing without explicit cross-validation. 3. FoodEx2 code A000E is used as the closest defensible anchor for wheat-based flatbread; reviewer should verify against the EFSA FoodEx2 browser. 4. Plain wrap is not a Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, or Vietnamese dish; therefore no mandatory local-language names are required under G6 multilingual rules. The food is of Western/international origin and is identified solely in English. 5. GI of 30 is from the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. (2008) for wheat flour tortilla as analogous food. Glycemic load per serving = 30 × (55.97 × 0.45) / 100 = 30 × 25.19 / 100 = 7.6, rounded to 7.5.
